UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile within the AgriGrow AI application, storing general user information and preferences. Authentication details like passwords are not stored here.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

preferredLanguage.description: The user's preferred language for application interactions, especially for the Agri-Bot.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

FarmField.title: FarmField
FarmField.description: Represents an individual farm field managed by a user, including its characteristics and geographical location. This entity is central to tracking and managing agricultural plots.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FarmField entity.

userId.description: Reference to UserProfile, indicating which user owns this farm field. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N FarmField)

name.description: A user-defined name for the farm field (e.g., 'North Plot', 'Field A').

cropType.description: The current type of crop planted in the field.

area.description: The area of the farm field.

areaUnit.description: The unit of measurement for the field's area (e.g., 'acres', 'hectares', 'sq_meters').

soilPH.description: The current soil pH level of the field.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude of the field for location-based services like weather.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude of the field for location-based services like weather.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the farm field record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the farm field record was last updated.

YieldHistory.title: YieldHistory
YieldHistory.description: Records historical yield data for a specific farm field, used for analysis and performance tracking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the YieldHistory entity.

farmFieldId.description: Reference to FarmField to which this yield history belongs. (Relationship: FarmField 1:N YieldHistory)

harvestDate.description: The date when the harvest was completed.

actualYieldAmount.description: The actual amount of yield harvested.

yieldUnit.description: The unit of measurement for the actual yield (e.g., 'kg', 'bushels', 'tons').

cropTypeAtHarvest.description: The type of crop harvested in this specific instance.

notes.description: Any additional notes or observations related to this harvest.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the yield history record was created.

YieldForecast.title: YieldForecast
YieldForecast.description: Stores AI-generated predictions for upcoming harvests, along with explanations and recommendations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the YieldForecast entity.

farmFieldId.description: Reference to FarmField for which this yield forecast applies. (Relationship: FarmField 1:N YieldForecast)

forecastDate.description: The date for which the yield forecast is made.

yieldUnit.description: The unit of measurement for the predicted yield (e.g., 'kg', 'bushels', 'tons').

predictionModelVersion.description: The version of the AI model used to generate this forecast.

explanation.description: An explanation from the AI detailing the factors influencing the yield prediction (Explainable AI Insights).

recommendation.description: Actionable advice generated by the AI based on the forecast and field data (Generative AI Recommendations).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the yield forecast was generated.

MeteorologicalData.title: MeteorologicalData
MeteorologicalData.description: Captures real-time meteorological conditions for a specific farm field, integrated from external weather services.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MeteorologicalData entity.

farmFieldId.description: Reference to FarmField for which this meteorological data is relevant. (Relationship: FarmField 1:N MeteorologicalData)

timestamp.description: The exact date and time the meteorological data was recorded.

temperatureCelsius.description: The temperature in Celsius at the field's location.

humidityPercentage.description: The humidity percentage at the field's location.

windSpeedMetersPerSec.description: The wind speed in meters per second at the field's location.

precipitationMillimeters.description: The amount of precipitation in millimeters at the field's location.

alerts.description: A list of dynamic alerts generated based on the weather conditions (e.g., 'Frost Warning', 'Irrigation Recommended').

source.description: The source of the meteorological data (e.g., 'OpenWeather').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the meteorological data record was created.

SoilReport.title: SoilReport
SoilReport.description: Stores extracted metrics from scanned physical soil lab reports, processed by AI Vision Suite.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SoilReport entity.

farmFieldId.description: Reference to FarmField to which this soil report belongs. (Relationship: FarmField 1:N SoilReport)

scanDate.description: The date when the soil report was scanned and processed.

reportImageUri.description: URI pointing to the stored image of the physical soil lab report.

extractedPH.description: The pH value extracted from the soil report.

extractedNitrogen.description: The Nitrogen (N) content extracted from the soil report.

extractedPhosphorus.description: The Phosphorus (P) content extracted from the soil report.

extractedPotassium.description: The Potassium (K) content extracted from the soil report.

extractedMoisturePercentage.description: The moisture percentage extracted from the soil report.

rawTextContent.description: The raw text content extracted from the scanned soil report, if available.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the soil report record was created.

DiseaseDetectionReport.title: DiseaseDetectionReport
DiseaseDetectionReport.description: Records AI Vision Suite's findings on detected plant diseases or pests and provides a recommended treatment plan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DiseaseDetectionReport entity.

farmFieldId.description: Reference to FarmField where the disease or pest was detected. (Relationship: FarmField 1:N DiseaseDetectionReport)

detectionDate.description: The date when the disease or pest was detected.

plantImageUri.description: URI pointing to the stored image of the affected plant or area.

detectedDisease.description: The name or type of the detected disease or pest.

confidenceScore.description: A numerical score (0.0 to 1.0) indicating the AI's confidence in the detection.

treatmentPlan.description: A 3-part treatment plan recommended by the AI for the detected issue.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the disease detection report was created.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. The document ID `{userId}` must match the `id` field within the UserProfile document and `request.auth.uid`. Data is private to the user.

Collection of farm fields owned by a specific user. The `{userId}` wildcard must match `request.auth.uid`. Each document includes the `userId` field, denormalizing ownership for authorization independence. The document ID `{farmFieldId}` must match the `id` field within the FarmField document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this farm field, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: farmFieldId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific farm field.

definition.description: Records historical yield data for a specific farm field. Each document includes the `userId` field (denormalized for authorization independence) and `farmFieldId` for direct linking. The `{userId}` wildcard must match `request.auth.uid`. The document ID `{yieldHistoryId}` must match the `id` field within the YieldHistory document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent farm field, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: farmFieldId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent farm field.

params.name: yieldHistoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific yield history record.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated yield predictions for a specific farm field. Each document includes the `userId` field (denormalized for authorization independence) and `farmFieldId` for direct linking. The `{userId}` wildcard must match `request.auth.uid`. The document ID `{yieldForecastId}` must match the `id` field within the YieldForecast document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent farm field, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: farmFieldId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent farm field.

params.name: yieldForecastId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific yield forecast record.

definition.description: Captures real-time meteorological conditions for a specific farm field. Each document includes the `userId` field (denormalized for authorization independence) and `farmFieldId` for direct linking. The `{userId}` wildcard must match `request.auth.uid`. The document ID `{dataId}` must match the `id` field within the MeteorologicalData document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent farm field, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: farmFieldId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent farm field.

params.name: dataId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific meteorological data record.

definition.description: Stores extracted metrics from scanned physical soil lab reports for a specific farm field. Each document includes the `userId` field (denormalized for authorization independence) and `farmFieldId` for direct linking. The `{userId}` wildcard must match `request.auth.uid`. The document ID `{reportId}` must match the `id` field within the SoilReport document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent farm field, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: farmFieldId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent farm field.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific soil report record.

definition.description: Records AI Vision Suite findings on detected plant diseases or pests for a specific farm field. Each document includes the `userId` field (denormalized for authorization independence) and `farmFieldId` for direct linking. The `{userId}` wildcard must match `request.auth.uid`. The document ID `{reportId}` must match the `id` field within the DiseaseDetectionReport document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent farm field, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: farmFieldId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent farm field.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific disease detection report record.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for AgriGrow AI prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access patterns by adhering strictly to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and QAPs (Query As Permissions). All user-specific data is rooted under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection, followed by a hierarchical structure for `FarmField` and its related sub-entities.

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization Strategy):**
1.  **User Profiles:** `UserProfile` documents are stored at `/users/{userId}`. Ownership is inherent in the path (`request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}`). The `id` field within the `UserProfile` document should always match the `userId` wildcard.
2.  **Farm Fields:** `FarmField` documents are stored as a subcollection under the specific user: `/users/{userId}/farmFields/{farmFieldId}`. Each `FarmField` document explicitly includes the `userId` field (as already defined in its schema). This denormalization means that security rules for `FarmField` can directly check `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `/users/{userId}` document. This enables atomic writes and simplifies rule evaluation.
3.  **Nested Data (YieldHistory, Forecasts, Weather, Soil Reports, Disease Reports):** All entities directly related to a `FarmField` (e.g., `YieldHistory`, `YieldForecast`, `MeteorologicalData`, `SoilReport`, `DiseaseDetectionReport`) are nested as subcollections under their respective `FarmField`: `/users/{userId}/farmFields/{farmFieldId}/<subcollection>/{docId}`. **CRITICALLY**, for full authorization independence, each document within these sub-subcollections (e.g., `yieldHistory`, `yieldForecasts`) **MUST include a denormalized `userId` field**. Although the `farmFieldId` is present in their schemas, relying solely on it would still require a `get()` on the parent `FarmField` document to determine its owner (`userId`). By denormalizing `userId` into *every* document in these sub-subcollections, security rules for these documents can evaluate ownership directly (`resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid`) without any `get()` calls, thus enabling atomic operations and simplifying rules significantly. The `farmFieldId` field in these documents further reinforces the link to the specific field.

**QAPs (Query As Permissions):**
This hierarchical structure inherently supports Query As Permissions. Users can only query their own data because the `userId` wildcard in the path will always be equated to `request.auth.uid`. For example:
*   To retrieve all farm fields for the current user, the client queries `/users/$(request.auth.uid)/farmFields`. The security rules ensure only documents where `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` are returned (or allowed to be listed).
*   To retrieve yield history for a specific farm field, the client queries `/users/$(request.auth.uid)/farmFields/{farmFieldId}/yieldHistory`. The rules, leveraging the denormalized `userId` within the `yieldHistory` document, confirm access without additional lookups.

This design ensures that all `list` operations are implicitly secure, as the query path itself dictates access based on the authenticated user's ID, and individual document rules confirm ownership via denormalized fields.

**Addressing User Feedback ('Deleted fields showing on home page'):**
The live update requirement for 'my field section' (FarmField) on the home page (dashboard) is directly supported by this structure. Firestore's real-time listeners, when applied to a query like `/users/$(request.auth.uid)/farmFields`, will automatically reflect additions, deletions, and updates to the `FarmField` documents in real-time, facilitating dynamic UI updates as requested by the user.

Regarding the 'firebase setup process failed and reinitiate', this refers to an operational setup task outside the scope of data structure design. The provided data structure forms a solid foundation for such a setup.
